title: zapoteca formosa subsp. schottii
intro: Zapoteca formosa subsp. schottii, also known as the Schott's Zapoteca, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its alkaloid and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Zapoteca formosa subsp. schottii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations often involve decoctions of the plant's leaves and roots, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of infusions or poultices.
